title: The particle content of the Standard Model: three generations of fermions (quarks, in p…
description: The particle content of the Standard Model: three generations of fermions (quarks, in purple, and leptons, in blue), the gauge bosons mediating the electromagnetic, weak, and strong interactions (red), and the Higgs boson responsible for electroweak symmetry breaking (gold, centre)

title: Representative tree-level diagrams for four-top production: QCD-mediated gluon exchange…
description: Representative tree-level diagrams for four-top production: QCD-mediated gluon exchange (left) and Higgs-mediated exchange (right). The right-hand diagram proceeds through the top-Higgs Yukawa coupling, so physics beyond the Standard Model that modifies this coupling alters the four-top cross-section; this is a potential origin of the process's BSM sensitivity

title: The CERN accelerator complex and its injector chain feeding the Large Hadron Collider,…
description: The CERN accelerator complex and its injector chain feeding the Large Hadron Collider, with the four main experimental interaction points indicated. The ATLAS detector sits at one of these four points

title: The ATLAS detector
description: The ATLAS detector. (a) Cutaway view showing the concentric arrangement of the Inner Detector, electromagnetic and hadronic calorimeters, solenoid and toroid magnets, and the Muon Spectrometer. (b) The ATLAS coordinate system overlaid on the same detector: the momentum $\vec {p}$ of a reconstructed object is described by its transverse component $p_{\mathrm {T}}$, pseudorapidity $\eta $, and azimuthal angle $\phi $ (figure produced by the author, inspired by A.\ Visive)

title: Post-fit distribution of the graph-neural-network discriminant in the signal region of…
description: Post-fit distribution of the graph-neural-network discriminant in the signal region of the ATLAS four-top observation at $\sqrt {s} = 13\,\mathrm {TeV}$ with $140\,\mathrm {fb}^{-1}$. The four-top signal ($t\bar {t}t\bar {t}$, red) accumulates at high GNN score over the associated-production and instrumental backgrounds; this separation drove the $6.1\sigma $ observation

title: Axis-group map of the workbench: all fifteen axis-group letters arranged into their six…
description: Axis-group map of the workbench: all fifteen axis-group letters arranged into their six categories. Upper tier: study framing (G) and data treatment (D). Model architecture block (left-to-right): tokenizer (T); positional encoding (E); pre-encoder (P); encoder block containing attention (A), physics-informed biases (B), and feed-forward network (F); classifier head (C). Lower tier: shared parameter blocks K, M, S; model-size hyperparameters H; training protocol R, L. Group colours follow the canonical workbench palette; the full per-axis reference is available online

title: Marginal AUROC by PID mode
description: Marginal AUROC by PID mode

title: D1 feature set: full four-vector [0,1,2,3] vs.\ three-vector [1,2,3] (no energy)
description: D1 feature set: full four-vector [0,1,2,3] vs.\ three-vector [1,2,3] (no energy)

title: D2 MET treatment, marginalised over D1 and D3
description: D2 MET treatment, marginalised over D1 and D3

title: D3 token ordering, restricted to D2\,=\,false ( per bar)
description: D3 token ordering, restricted to D2\,=\,false ($n=6$ per bar)

title: AUROC across the PID encoding mode (T1-a, columns) embedding dimension (T1-b, rows) grid
description: AUROC across the PID encoding mode (T1-a, columns) $\times $ embedding dimension (T1-b, rows) grid. One-hot is invariant to dimension by construction; the dim=16 and dim=32 cells are left blank for that row

title: Per-event kinematic aggregates (, mean , , -spread) vs.\ the identity-minus-raw signal-…
description: Per-event kinematic aggregates ($\Sigma p_T$, mean $|\eta |$, $\Sigma E$, $\phi $-spread) vs.\ the identity-minus-raw signal-score margin, for PID-helped (blue) and PID-hurt (vermillion)

title: Particle multiplicity per event, by outcome category
description: Particle multiplicity per event, by outcome category. Medians agree within $\pm 1$ particle (both correct: 10; PID-helped, PID-hurt, both wrong: 9)

title: Tokenizer family (T1): mean test AUROC by family (left) and validation AUROC over train…
description: Tokenizer family (T1): mean test AUROC by family (left) and validation AUROC over training (right)

title: ROC curves for the three tokenizer families
description: ROC curves for the three tokenizer families. Individual seed curves are shown for raw and binned; the mean curve is shown for identity. The performance ordering is consistent across all background rejection values

title: Per-event outcome comparison between the best raw and best identity tokenizer models on…
description: Per-event outcome comparison between the best raw and best identity tokenizer models on the test set ($N=30{,}207$). Left: scatter of per-event signal scores; colours indicate outcome category. Events above the diagonal (identity score $>$ raw score) tend to benefit from the PID label. Right: fraction of events in each category. The net asymmetry (9.0% PID-helped vs.\ 6.0% PID-hurt) accounts for the identity tokenizer's overall AUROC advantage

title: Per-event observables compared between PID-helped and PID-hurt categories
description: Per-event observables compared between PID-helped and PID-hurt categories. (a) Particle multiplicity (violin). (b) Four event-aggregate kinematic observables (scatter). Both views overlap with no visible kinematic structure separating the two categories

title: PID embedding mode (T1-a) and embedding dimension (T1-b) for the identity tokenizer
description: PID embedding mode (T1-a) and embedding dimension (T1-b) for the identity tokenizer. (a) The marginal AUROC by mode, pooled over all dimensions: the three modes lie within $\sim 0.003$ AUROC of one another, comparable to the per-cell seed scatter. (b) The $\texttt {T1-a} \times \texttt {T1-b}$ grid resolves the comparison against dimension: one_hot is invariant to dimension by construction (dimension is set to the number of particle types), and the spread across the full grid is $\sim 0.004$ AUROC

title: Data-treatment axes for the identity tokenizer, shown as three single-panel bar charts…
description: Data-treatment axes for the identity tokenizer, shown as three single-panel bar charts in the same style as the left panel of Figure. All three axes are near-null effects ($|\Delta | < 0.003$ AUROC)

title: Full-combination B1 = lorentz+typepair+sm+global
description: Full-combination B1 = lorentz+typepair+sm+global

title: B1 = none baseline
description: B1 = none baseline

title: lorentz kan edges splines dR
description: $q = \Delta R$

title: lorentz kan edges splines logkt
description: $q = \log k_T$

title: lorentz kan edges splines logm2
lorentz kan edges splines z
description: $q = z$

title: Mean edge amplitude per input over all hidden units
description: Mean edge amplitude per input over all hidden units

title: Four input edges into hidden unit
description: Four input edges into hidden unit $j = 6$

title: Exp.\ 5A seed-mean test AUROC for all six B1 configurations, with per-seed spread
description: Exp.\ 5A seed-mean test AUROC for all six B1 configurations, with per-seed spread. Dashed line marks the none baseline mean

title: Per-type mean attention weights for the unbiased baseline (left) and the full-combinati…
description: Per-type mean attention weights for the unbiased baseline (left) and the full-combination bias stack (right), averaged over all heads, all layers, three seeds, and 2000 validation events. Tick order (both axes): jet, $b$-jet, $\gamma $, $e^{+}$, $e^{-}$, $\mu ^{+}$, $\mu ^{-}$. Padding tokens excluded. Colour scale (teal sequential) is shared across the two panels for direct comparability

title: Schematic contrast between a multi-layer perceptron (left) and a Kolmogorov-Arnold netw…
description: Schematic contrast between a multi-layer perceptron (left) and a Kolmogorov-Arnold network (right), reproduced from Liu et al.. An MLP places fixed activation functions on the nodes and learnable weights on the edges; a KAN places learnable univariate activation functions on the edges and a plain sum on the nodes. The shallow formulas underneath state the universal approximation theorem (MLP) and the Kolmogorov-Arnold representation theorem (KAN) that each architecture parameterises

title: Exp.\ 5B seed-mean test AUROC for all 20 (feature set, MLP type, gating) cells, with pe…
description: Exp.\ 5B seed-mean test AUROC for all 20 (feature set, MLP type, gating) cells, with per-seed spread. Left panel: sparse gating off. Right panel: sparse gating on. KAN (gold) consistently meets or exceeds the standard MLP (near-black); sparse gating has no visible effect at any feature-set size

title: Learned univariate splines on every edge from each reduced-panel input feature to the e…
description: Learned univariate splines $\varphi _{q, j}$ on every edge from each reduced-panel input feature $q$ to the eight hidden units $j = 0\ldots 7$ in the first KAN layer, anchor checkpoint (four-feature panel, gate-off, seed 314). Each curve is the univariate function the network applies to a single feature before the node-level sum, read directly off the trained B-spline coefficients with no partial-dependence assumption on the other inputs. The vertical scale is the pre-gate contribution in arbitrary units; the deployed amplitude in the attention logit is uniformly scaled by $\tanh (g) = 0.382$ for this checkpoint. The horizontal axis is the model's z-score-normalised input scale, whose span differs per feature

title: Per-node reading of the same anchor KAN checkpoint
description: Per-node reading of the same anchor KAN checkpoint. Left: the four reduced-panel input edges $\varphi _{q, j = 6}$ feeding one representative hidden unit, labelled by feature. Right: the per-feature edge amplitude $\mathrm {std}(\varphi _{q, j})$ averaged over the eight hidden units, with the spread across units shown as the error bar. The two panels read the same trained B-spline coefficients as Figure grouped by hidden unit rather than by input feature

title: Per-feature sparse gate values for the Exp.\ 5B sparse-on KAN runs
description: Per-feature sparse gate values $\sigma (\text {feature gate})$ for the Exp.\ 5B sparse-on KAN runs. Each row is a B1-L1 feature panel (the single scalars $\Delta R$ and $m^{2}$ at the top, the reduced four-feature and full six-feature combinations below); each column is one scalar. Active cells give the converged gate value; blank cells mark scalars not supplied to that panel. All values stay near the $0.5$ initialisation, so the model admits every supplied scalar on roughly equal footing and prunes none

title: Per-cell drift of the converged seed-mean type-pair table from its reference initialisa…
description: Per-cell drift of the converged seed-mean type-pair table from its reference initialisation, evaluated on the $7\times 7$ active species block. Left: binary, free. Right: fixed_coupling, free. The two panels share a single gold-brown diverging colour scale centred on zero, so the cell-by-cell movement of the two priors can be compared directly. The two frozen cells (binary and fixed_coupling) are omitted: their per-cell drift is zero by construction (Table )

title: Per-layer attention entropy on a held-out batch
description: Per-layer attention entropy on a held-out batch

title: Per-head over training, averaged across heads and seeds
description: Per-head $\lambda _h$ over training, averaged across heads and seeds

title: Performance lens for Experiment 1 (A3)
description: Performance lens for Experiment 1 (A3). Left: seed-spread test AUROC for both attention types in the matched chapter cell (A4\,=\,none, B1\,=\,lorentz_scalar); each point is one seed and the horizontal bar shows the per-cell mean. The differential bar pools the three A3-a variants from exp6b ($n=12$); the standard bar is the matched A3-a\,=\,shared cell ($n=3$). Right: validation AUROC training curves for the same matched cell over fifty epochs, plotting individual seed traces with the per-attention-type mean overlaid. Both attention types peak near $0.835$ within ten to fifteen epochs and decline monotonically to $\approx 0.790$ by epoch fifty; the reported test AUROC of $0.832$ to $0.833$ is read at the epoch of peak validation and sits slightly below the validation peak because the test and validation splits are statistically independent at fixed read-out epoch

title: Differential-attention diagnostics for the interpretability lens of Experiment 1 (A4\,=…
description: Differential-attention diagnostics for the interpretability lens of Experiment 1 (A4\,=\,none, B1\,=\,lorentz_scalar, three seeds). (a) $\lambda _h$ converges to stable non-zero values across heads, layers, and seeds, with per-layer means rising monotonically with depth. (b) Deep-layer entropy is lower for differential attention than for standard attention; the two curves cross between L1 and L2 and the final-layer gap is approximately $0.7$ nats

title: Performance lens for Experiment 2, A4 effect
description: Performance lens for Experiment 2, A4 effect. Left: mean test AUROC heatmap for the A3 $\times $ A4 interaction across the 36 exp6a runs (marginalised over B1); A4\,=\,none is the best row at both attention types, and differential attention sits above standard attention in every A4 row. Right: validation AUROC training curves for the three A4 options at the matched cell A3\,=\,differential, B1\,=\,lorentz_scalar; the none curve pools the twelve runs that share this cell across the chapter-6 sweep groups, the layernorm and rmsnorm curves are the three seeds of the matched cell within exp6a. All three curves peak near epoch ten to fifteen and decline thereafter; the ordering between the three options is established by epoch twenty and is preserved across the decline. The heatmap reports test AUROC read at the epoch of peak validation AUROC, so the heatmap values sit slightly below the visible peaks of the right-panel curves because the test split is independent of the validation split on which the read-out epoch is selected

title: Mean AUROC heatmap for the A3 B1 interaction, marginalised over A4 (Experiment 2 runs)
description: Mean AUROC heatmap for the A3 $\times $ B1 interaction, marginalised over A4 (Experiment 2 runs). The Lorentz-scalar bias provides a clear gain for differential attention ($+0.0033$) but essentially no gain for standard attention ($+0.0002$)

title: Seed-spread AUROC for the three A3-a differential bias modes within Experiment 3 (A4\,=…
description: Seed-spread AUROC for the three A3-a differential bias modes within Experiment 3 (A4\,=\,none, B1\,=\,lorentz_scalar). The three modes are indistinguishable within seed variation

title: Test AUROC versus model size on a log parameter-count axis, one line per task (4t vs
description: Test AUROC versus model size on a log parameter-count axis, one line per task (4t vs. background, 4t vs. ttH, multiclass). Each preset is positioned at its actual parameter count; the H10 preset label appears as an italic-gray sub-tick. Shaded $\pm 1\sigma $ bands give the seed spread. The y-axis is locked to $[0.80, 0.90]$ to share scale with Figure. The plateau at d64_L6 is visible in all three tasks

title: Training compute to convergence versus model size, with both axes on a log scale: the x…
description: Training compute to convergence versus model size, with both axes on a log scale: the x-axis is the actual parameter count and the y-axis is training FLOPs. Training FLOPs are $3 \times f_\mathrm {evt}^\mathrm {analytic} \times N_\mathrm {events/epoch} \times e^\star $, with the factor 3 the forward$+$backward convention of Kaplan et al., $N_\mathrm {events/epoch} = 241{,}658$, and $e^\star $ the best-validation-AUROC epoch. The shaded band propagates the seed spread on $e^\star $; boxed top labels give the mean best epoch $\pm $ std per preset, with the H10 preset shown as an italic-gray sub-tick under each parameter-count tick

title: Test AUROC versus per-batch inference latency at batch size 512 (linear x, \,ms), one l…
description: Test AUROC versus per-batch inference latency at batch size 512 (linear x, $[0, 2.5]$\,ms), one line per task with $\pm 1\sigma $ bands along the latency-ordered curve. Boxed top labels give parameter counts; the y-axis matches Figure. Points up and to the left are Pareto-preferable; d64_L6 sits at the elbow

title: marginals family
marginals top10
Mean pairwise Cramér's V between axis families on the 881-run primary cohort
description: Mean pairwise Cramér's V between axis families on the 881-run primary cohort. The diagonal is blanked and the colour scale is the chapter navy. Off-diagonal cells below 0.2 indicate near-independence at the family level

title: Per-axis AUROC range on the 881-run primary cohort
description: Per-axis AUROC range on the 881-run primary cohort. (a) The ten axes with the largest range, coloured by family. The leading axes are training-protocol (R: warmup R6, batch R4, learning rate R2) and model-size (H) axes, whose ranges reflect under-trained runs at the boundary rather than architectural sensitivity. (b) Mean range per family across the axes passing the five-runs-per-level gate. For on/off families such as backbone sharing S and the MIA pre-encoder P, the bar is the enable-versus-disable AUROC gap, so the panel mixes across-setting and use-versus-not-use spreads. G is constant on this single-task cohort and L affects logging only, so both carry no measurable range

title: Out-of-fold predicted (horizontal) versus actual (vertical) AUROC for the XGBoost surro…
description: Out-of-fold predicted (horizontal) versus actual (vertical) AUROC for the XGBoost surrogate under five-fold cross-validation, with the $y=x$ reference dashed. The cross-validated rank correlation is Spearman $\rho \,=\,0.851\,\pm \,0.047$. The cohort floor sits at AUROC 0.75 because the failed label excludes every lower run

title: Normalised mean per axis family, sorted descending
description: Normalised mean $|\text {SHAP}|$ per axis family, sorted descending. The tokeniser family accounts for 44.0% of the surrogate's attribution, driven by the PID embedding mode T1-a; the model-size family H and the positional-encoding family E follow at 22.6% and 6.0%. The training-protocol family R sits fourth at 5.2%

title: Per-level mean SHAP for two readable training-protocol axes (left: warmup steps R6; rig…
description: Per-level mean SHAP for two readable training-protocol axes (left: warmup steps R6; right: batch size R4). Navy bars indicate a net positive contribution to predicted AUROC and soft red a net negative one. The two panels share an $x$-range so the scale is directly comparable. These axes are illustrative of the training-protocol response; the tokeniser, model-size and positional-encoding families carry the largest attribution (Figure )

title: Predicted (horizontal) versus three-seed actual (vertical) AUROC for the nine proposed…
description: Predicted (horizontal) versus three-seed actual (vertical) AUROC for the nine proposed configurations, with vertical error bars at $\sigma $ (zero for the single-seed Optuna markers). The dashed grey diagonal is $y=x$. The cyan horizontal line marks the MIA reference at 0.8512, which was not proposed by any strategy and therefore has no predicted value. Surrogate-1 sits closest to the reference line. The three Optuna proposals coincide at a single point and share one label

title: ROC curves for MIA (blue) and the physics baseline (grey), both solid; the shaded band…
description: ROC curves for MIA (blue) and the physics baseline (grey), both solid; the shaded band on MIA is $\pm $1\,std across five seeds. Left: full range; right: zoom on the low-false-positive-rate region. Markers indicate the working points $1/B = 50$, $100$, and $1000$ (labelled in the zoom panel). Task: 4t vs.\ ttH+ttW+ttWW+ttZ

title: Signal efficiency (TPR) as a function of background rejection (, logarithmic axis) for…
description: Signal efficiency (TPR) as a function of background rejection ($1/\mathrm {FPR}$, logarithmic axis) for MIA (blue, shaded band) and the physics baseline (grey), distinguished by colour. Dot markers on both curves indicate the three working points $1/B = 50$, $100$, and $1000$. The gap between the two curves widens towards high background rejection

title: Discriminant score distributions for signal (4t) and background (ttH+ttW+ttWW+ttZ) even…
description: Discriminant score distributions for signal (4t) and background (ttH+ttW+ttWW+ttZ) events, comparing MIA (top) and the physics baseline (bottom). Signal and background are each normalised to unit area, so the panels compare distribution shapes; relative heights are not relative event rates. A clearer separation is visible for MIA

title: Illustrative expected significance as a function of score threshold for MIA (blue) and…
description: Illustrative expected significance $Z$ as a function of score threshold for MIA (blue) and the physics baseline (grey), both solid, computed from Eq. with $\sigma (4t) = \SI {12}{\femto \barn }$, $\sigma (\mathrm {bkg}) = \SI {111}{\femto \barn }$, $\mathcal {L} = \SI {520}{\per \femto \barn }$, and a 20 flat background systematic. Horizontal lines mark the $Z = 3$ and $Z = 5$ thresholds. The estimate is not a profile-likelihood fit and is illustrative only
